seed_flags.clause_title: The maximum aggregate liability of one party to the other under or in
seed_flags.snippet: The maximum aggregate liability of one party to the other under or in connection with this Agreement shall be limited to an amount equal to ______. Neither party shall be liable to the other for any loss of profits, rev…
seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

augmented_flags.clause_title: EIGHTH. Both parties agree to treat any scientific and technical
augmented_flags.snippet: EIGHTH. Both parties agree to treat any scientific and technical information received from the other party during the collaboration as confidential, ensuring limited circulation of said information and assuming responsibility for this obligation being fulfilled by all personnel who require access under this agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position under POS-11. Specifically, it does not exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, it does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). While the clause is generally acceptable, it requires confirmation that it aligns with UoA's Acceptable tier, which allows confidentiality obligations limited in time (e.g., 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure). Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended for further review.

augmented_flags.clause_title: This duty of confidentiality will persist indefinitely following the
augmented_flags.snippet: This duty of confidentiality will persist indefinitely following the termination of this contract.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but specifies an indefinite duration, which is not aligned with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. UoA's Preferred position under POS-11 limits confidentiality obligations to a maximum of 5 years post-completion, and the Acceptable position allows for 5–7 years. Indefinite confidentiality obligations are outside the Acceptable tier and require escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential renegotiation.

augmented_flags.snippet: NINTH. The parties undertake to fully comply with the data protection obligations contained in the REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL on the protection of natural persons about the processing of pe…

augmented_flags.snippet: The parties have the right to request access to their personal data, rectification or deletion, as well as the limitation of its treatment, to oppose it and the portability of their data.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses data protection rights, which is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: TENTH. The parties expressly accept the content and ethical values
augmented_flags.snippet: TENTH. The parties expressly accept the content and ethical values established in the Code of Conduct and Anticorruption Policy of the other party, whose content they know, formally committing to putting it into practice in each one of the decisions, processes, and activities developed in collaboration with the party, as well as not carrying out any practice that in any way may lead to a violation of applicable laws or regulations related to corruption.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses ethical values and anticorruption policies, which are not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and requires further review to determine its appropriateness.

augmented_flags.clause_title: Failure to comply with the provisions of this clause, as well as any
augmented_flags.snippet: Failure to comply with the provisions of this clause, as well as any unethical behavior that may arise during the relationship of the parties must be reported immediately, will be cause for resolution, and, where appropriate, will result in demanding lawful compensation for damages that may arise.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses a topic (unethical behavior and its consequences) that is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. There is no guidance or standard template language for this type of provision, and it does not align with any of the listed topics or keywords.

augmented_flags.clause_title: ELEVENTH. The collaboration relationship provided for in this agreement
augmented_flags.snippet: ELEVENTH. The collaboration relationship provided for in this agreement excludes any employment relationship between the parties.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the exclusion of an employment relationship between the parties, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or a UoA Standard Template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policies and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: The parties undertake to resolve amicably any disagreement that may
augmented_flags.snippet: The parties undertake to resolve amicably any disagreement that may arise in the development of this Collaboration Agreement. In case of divergences in the interpretation and fulfillment of the same or the specific agreements or contracts that result, the Courts and Tribunals of the parties' headquarters will be the only ones with competency, the parties expressly waiving to any other jurisdiction that may correspond.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses the topic of Governing Law and Jurisdiction (UoA Position #POS-16). The clause specifies that the Courts and Tribunals of the parties' headquarters will have exclusive jurisdiction, which is inconsistent with both the Preferred (New Zealand) and Acceptable (Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, Singapore, or non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state) positions. Exclusive jurisdiction in unspecified locations introduces significant legal risk and falls outside the Acceptable tier. Escalation to General Counsel is required.

augmented_flags.clause_title: The maximum aggregate liability of one party to the other under or in
augmented_flags.snippet: The maximum aggregate liability of one party to the other under or in connection with this Agreement shall be limited to an amount equal to ______. Neither party shall be liable to the other for any loss of profits, revenue, savings, business, goodwill, or for any consequential, indirect, incidental, or special damages of any kind under or in connection with this Agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions, which is covered under UoA Position #POS-07. While the clause explicitly excludes consequential, indirect, and other specified losses, it does not specify a monetary cap for the maximum aggregate liability. This omission prevents full alignment with the Preferred tier, which requires a cap of the lesser of the contract value or NZD$500,000. However, the clause aligns with the Acceptable tier, provided the total liability limit (including indemnities) is in line with Research Contract Delegations. The clause does not include carve-outs for fraud, wilful default, personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, or confidentiality, which are acceptable exceptions under the UoA Positions. The clause should be reviewed further to ensure compliance with the Research Contract Delegations.

augmented_flags.clause_title: Title: a university constituted under the University of Auckland Act
augmented_flags.snippet: Title: a university constituted under the University of Auckland Act 1961
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the University's legal constitution under the University of Auckland Act 1961, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or standard template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the Positions document.
